[BTG+96]  Autonomous entities in distributed virtual environments

Balet.mmm96 (In proceedings)
Author(s)Balet, Olivier and Torguet, Patrice and Gaildrat, Véronique and Caubet, René
Title« Autonomous entities in distributed virtual environments »
InMultimedia Modeling Multiconferences: Towards The Information Superhighway
Page(s)457--471
Year1996

Abstract
Developing distributed virtual environments is a complex time consuming task. In order to develop such environments the programmer has to be proficient in network, graphics, device handlers and user interface programming. Moreover, network based programs are inherently more difficult to program and debug than sequential ones. In order to simplify this task, we have developed VIPER (VIrtuality Programming EnviRonment) which enables the rapid and easy development of distributed virtual environments. This paper will first describe our distributed virtual environment model. Then we will describe the software architecture of a VIPER entity by presenting DeM2onE (DEclarative Multimodal MOdelliNg Entity), its behavioural model and its features.
